title: Caesalpinia pomaria
taxonomy: Caesalpinia pomaria is classified within the family Fabaceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as legumes. This family includes over 19,000 species, many of which are economically important for their edible seeds, pods, and leaves. As a member of the Fabaceae family, Caesalpinia pomaria is also part of the subfamily Caesalpinioideae, which includes species with large, showy flowers and woody stems. The Caesalpinioideae subfamily is further divided into several tribes, with Caesalpinia pomaria likely belonging to one of these groups. Within the tribe, the exact classification of Caesalpinia pomaria can be further refined based on its specific characteristics and relationships to other species.

intro: Caesalpinia pomaria is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and digestive issues, due to its potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties.
